response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with 15+ years of experience, but his background is overwhelmingly in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile development. C++ appears only in a brief role at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, with no evidence of production-grade C++ development using STL, multi-threading, or message-broker architectures at the depth this role demands. There is no indication of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 or SDL security practices, MQTT, or any of the industrial protocols listed as core or advantageous requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed systems experience (Dunnhumby micro-services, Northwind Labs geo-data platform) shows architectural awareness relevant to message-broker patterns
Consistent track record of high-ownership, multi-language polyglot engineering across embedded, web, and mobile domains spanning 15+ years
TDD discipline, CI/CD practices, and mentoring/community contributions (Dojos, workshops, open-source libraries) signal strong engineering culture fit
concerns: C++ proficiency is surface-level and dated — the only relevant C/C++ work is a 6-month NI role in 2014–2015 and a student thesis; no evidence of STL, concurrent C++ design, or production-scale C++ systems
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443-4-1 Secure Development Lifecycle, or any of the listed industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT, DNP 3.0)
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, React) is entirely misaligned with the core technical competencies required for this role
